rationale: v3. Earlier v2 (claimed 80 leaves) never actually got written — the file kept the v1 content. This version writes the real v2 spine and adds children for the smaller L2 specialties that previously had none, so every L2 specialty has at least one L3 child for the classifier to land on. Cardiology has 6 fellowships; oncology 6 tumor/treatment splits; psychiatry 5; derm 5; nephrology 3; neurosurgery 3; ortho 4; urology 2; ophth 3; ENT 3. Smaller specialties get 1-3 L3 leaves each.

uncovered_concerns: v3 is now sized properly — 110 L3 leaves across all 50 L2 specialties. Every L2 has ≥1 L3 child.

Several L2 specialties have only 1 L3 child because the specialty doesn't decompose further into named subspecialties at the level of L3 papers (e.g. anesthesiology, pain medicine, emergency medicine — they each get sub-domain L4 leaves instead).
L4 leaves live in level4/medicine.json with parent_l3 pointing to these slugs.
